title: Investigate test_api_endpoints suite timeout in basic CI
description:
## Summary
`bash scripts/ci/run_basic_ci.sh` and file-level `pytest extraction/tests/test_api_endpoints.py` can stall in the sandbox after the semantic endpoint metadata tests, while targeted endpoint tests pass individually.

## Reproduction
Run `timeout 180s env UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest extraction/tests/test_api_endpoints.py -vv --maxfail=1`. The run reaches `test_run_agent_semantic_endpoint_returns_support_and_strategy_metadata` and then emits no further progress before timeout.

## Expected
The file-level endpoint suite completes consistently so basic CI can run end-to-end locally and in GitHub.

## Actual
Targeted tests pass, but the file-level suite can stall under current sandbox execution.

## Collaboration Context
- Severity: medium
- Impact: medium
- Urgency: this_sprint
- Sprint: 2026-S03
- Roadmap: platform-workflow
- Area: backend

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stalling test interaction identified
- [ ] File-level endpoint suite completes under timeout
- [ ] `bash scripts/ci/run_basic_ci.sh` completes end-to-end

title: Create canonical query engine module
description:
## Goal
Make query a first-class canonical engine in `seocho/` so local SDK and server runtime share planning, execution, and answer-synthesis contracts.

## Scope
- In scope:
  - define canonical query boundaries under `seocho/query/`
  - move or wrap shared query planning/execution logic so local and server paths call the same engine contract
  - separate planner, executor, and answer-synthesis responsibilities clearly enough that parity tests can target them
- Out of scope:
  - major retrieval-policy redesign
  - new debate features
  - broad prompt or model experimentation unrelated to parity

## Collaboration Context
- Severity: high
- Impact: high
- Urgency: this_sprint
- Sprint: 2026-S03
- Roadmap: platform-workflow
- Area: backend

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Local SDK and server runtime query paths call shared canonical query logic for at least one core path.
- [ ] Planner, executor, and answer-synthesis responsibilities are no longer spread ambiguously across `client.py` and `extraction/*`.
- [ ] Tests compare local and server query behavior at the shared contract level.
- [ ] Public behavior changes, if any, are documented explicitly.
design: Target shape: seocho/query/{contracts,planner,executor,answering} with extraction runtime importing this engine instead of owning parallel query orchestration.
notes: Implemented first canonical query engine slice: added seocho/query/{contracts,planner,executor,answering}; local SDK query path now delegates planner/executor/answer synthesis through canonical modules; semantic evidence bundle helpers moved behind seocho.query.answering and server runtime imports shared helpers. title: Canonicalize runtime package boundary and plan extraction-to-runtime rename
description:
## Summary
Establish a long-horizon migration from the historically overloaded extraction/ package to a thinner runtime/server shell while keeping seocho/ as the canonical engine.

## Reproduction
The package name extraction/ now contains API transport, policy, server composition, and compatibility shims in addition to true ingestion code. This makes SDK-vs-runtime boundaries unclear and keeps historical naming drift alive.

## Expected
seocho/ is the canonical engine, runtime/server is the deployment shell, and extraction/ is either reduced to extraction-only concerns or removed behind a staged rename plan.

## Actual
extraction/ still acts as the de facto runtime package name even after canonical query/agent/ontology moves into seocho/*.

## Collaboration Context
- Severity: high
- Impact: high
- Urgency: this_sprint
- Sprint: 2026-S03
- Roadmap: platform-workflow
- Area: backend

title: bug: DNS resolve failure in SDK indexing pipeline (neo4j:7687)
description:
## Problem
Seocho.add() fails with 'Failed to DNS resolve address neo4j:7687' on all write operations.

Direct Neo4jGraphStore.write() works fine. Direct neo4j driver session works fine.
The issue is specifically in the SDK pipeline path: Seocho.add() → _LocalEngine.add() → IndexingPipeline.index() → GraphStore.write().

## Evidence
- store.write([...], database='neo4j') → SUCCESS
- store.query('MATCH ...', database='finderlpg') → SUCCESS (17060 nodes)
- s.add('text', database='neo4j') → FAIL: DNS resolve neo4j:7687
- s.add('text', database='finderlpg') → FAIL: DNS resolve neo4j:7687

## Hypothesis
IndexingPipeline may be creating a separate Neo4jGraphStore instance or the pipeline is passing different connection parameters to the driver.

## Impact
All E2E indexing fails. No data can be written through the SDK.

## Requested
Full code path trace from Seocho.add() to the actual Neo4j session.run() call. Find where 'neo4j:7687' hostname appears instead of 'localhost:7687'.
close_reason: Root cause: .env had NEO4J_URI=bolt://neo4j:7687 (Docker hostname). Fixed to bolt://localhost:7687. E2E now runs successfully (10/10 indexing, queries working).

notes:
FinDER 10-case local runtime diagnostic pass completed in two local-only batches: 20260415T231447Z (cases 001-005) and 20260415T231557Z (cases 006-010). Combined endpoint results: memory_chat 10/10 contains; semantic_reasoning_off/on 9/10 each; graph-scoped router_agent 9/10; platform_chat_semantic 9/10; debate_agent 3/6; memory_search 0/10 because it returns search payloads rather than synthesized answers. Good cases: finder_001, 004, 005, 007, 008, 009, 010 pass semantic/router/platform paths. Edge cases filed: seocho-gt4.1 for finder_006 support fact truncation around H100/A100 product identifiers; seocho-gt4.2 for debate no-data phrase variants on finder_002/003/006.
FinDER regression tests added after local diagnostics: runtime memory content_preview now preserves longer support sentences, semantic answer synthesis keeps product identifiers, debate no-data detector covers observed graph-agent phrase variants, and benchmark answer comparison treats scaled numeric units such as 1.31 million and 1,310,000 as equivalent. Focused validation: 17 pytest checks passed. Local-only diagnostics: 20260415T232634Z fixed finder_006 semantic/router/platform paths; 20260415T233346Z confirmed semantic paths for finder_002-006 all pass and exposed debate nondeterminism as the next structural risk.

title: Canonicalize SDK and runtime engine boundaries
description:
## Summary
Reduce local SDK and server runtime drift by making `seocho/` the canonical engine for indexing, query, agents, and ontology-derived artifacts while shrinking `extraction/` into HTTP transport, policy enforcement, and backward-compatible adapters.

## Reproduction
- The same user intent can take materially different code paths in local SDK mode and server runtime mode.
- Indexing has started to consolidate under `seocho/index`, but query, agent, and ontology logic still live across `seocho/`, `client.py`, and `extraction/` runtime modules.
- This makes parity testing, docs, and maintenance harder than they need to be.

## Expected
- `seocho/` owns canonical engine modules for indexing, query, agents, and ontology-derived artifact generation.
- `extraction/` owns FastAPI transport, policy checks, runtime provisioning hooks, and compatibility shims only.
- Local SDK and server runtime share the same planning and execution contracts, with parity tests at the shared boundaries.

## Actual
- Query is split across `seocho/query/*`, `seocho/client.py`, `extraction/semantic_query_flow.py`, and `extraction/memory_service.py`.
- Agent behavior is split across `seocho/agents.py`, `seocho/session.py`, `extraction/platform_agents.py`, `extraction/debate.py`, and route-specific orchestration.
- Ontology responsibilities are concentrated in `seocho/ontology.py` while related runtime artifact logic is also surfaced through client/runtime helpers.
- `extraction/agent_server.py` and `seocho/client.py` remain too large and mix orchestration with interface concerns.

## Collaboration Context
- Severity: high
- Impact: high
- Urgency: this_sprint
- Sprint: 2026-S03
- Roadmap: platform-workflow
- Area: backend

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canonical ownership for index/query/agent/ontology surfaces is documented and enforced in code layout.
- [ ] Query, agent, and ontology refactors have concrete child tasks with explicit merge gates.
- [ ] `seocho/client.py` and `extraction/agent_server.py` have follow-up decomposition tasks linked to this issue.
- [ ] A parity harness or equivalent shared validation gate is defined for local SDK vs server runtime behavior.
- [ ] Deferred cleanup risk in `extraction/` is documented instead of hidden.
design: Canonical direction: seocho/ owns engines; extraction/ becomes transport+policy+compatibility adapters. Sequence query -> agent -> ontology, with client/server split and extraction cleanup supporting that move.

Validation: py_compile, 21 focused agent/session tests, lint-agent-docs, git diff --check. Follow-up: extraction/agent_server remains outside canonical agent package for now.

title: Migration plan safety layer — dry-run, staged rollout, rollback

title: Split seocho client facade responsibilities
description:
## Goal
Decompose `seocho/client.py` into clearer local-engine, HTTP transport, and artifact-helper boundaries to reduce god-object drift.

## Scope
- In scope:
  - extract transport helpers, local-engine orchestration, and artifact-facing helpers into clearer modules or sections
  - reduce query/agent/ontology logic that is embedded only because `client.py` became the central facade
- Out of scope:
